stem: A mission enters the space mission pipeline. The probability of passing design review is between 65% and 83% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing testing is 70%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing launch is 81%. What is the overall probability that the mission is successful?

ground_truth.answer: P(successful) ranges from 0.369 to 0.471 as the design review pass rate varies from 65% to 83%. Point estimate: 0.42.

solution_steps: The mission must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the design review stage creates a 10.2 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the design review rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
